qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of this page?
qa_pairs.answer: This page provides instructions on how to set up and access Arbutus object storage using WinSCP.

qa_pairs.question: Which object storage client is discussed on this page for Arbutus object storage?
qa_pairs.answer: This page discusses WinSCP as one of the object storage clients available for Arbutus object storage.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I download and install WinSCP from?
qa_pairs.answer: WinSCP can be installed from https://winscp.net/.

qa_pairs.question: What file protocol should be selected when configuring a new session in WinSCP for Arbutus object storage?
qa_pairs.answer: You should select "Amazon S3" as the file protocol.

qa_pairs.question: What is the required host name for WinSCP configuration to access Arbutus object storage?
What port number should be used in the WinSCP configuration?
What kind of Access Key ID is required for WinSCP configuration?
qa_pairs.answer: A 20-digit access key ID is required.

qa_pairs.question: After initial setup, what advanced setting must be changed in WinSCP?
qa_pairs.answer: The "URL style:" setting must be changed from "Virtual Host" to "Path" within the 'Advanced...' settings under 'Environment' > 'S3' > 'Protocol options'.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to change the URL style to 'Path' in WinSCP?
qa_pairs.answer: This "Path" setting is important because otherwise WinSCP will not work, and you will see hostname resolution errors.

qa_pairs.question: What tasks can be performed using the WinSCP GUI after logging in?
qa_pairs.answer: After logging in, you can use the WinSCP GUI to create buckets and to transfer files.

qa_pairs.question: How can I set a file's Access Control List (ACL) in WinSCP?
qa_pairs.answer: You can set a file's ACL by right-clicking on the file in WinSCP.
